content: We can frame the linguistic teaching of 'reflexion' as a core metacognitive vocabulary term to guide LLM self-reflection mechanisms, where prompting an LLM to explicitly verbalize its reflexion process using the structured layered definitions of the term (reflect, reflect on errors, re-evaluate outputs) improves the consistency of its self-correction. This turns a basic high school English vocabulary definition into a structured prompt template for LLM self-reflection.
